title: Systems and methods for encrypting files
abstract: The disclosed computer-implemented method for encrypting files may include (i) detecting an event within a network that triggers an encryption of a file on the network, (ii) performing, in response to detecting the event, both encrypting the file to a file encryption key and encrypting the file encryption key to a public key of a source of the file, (iii) receiving, from a client, a file access request that includes the encrypted file encryption key, and (iv) transmitting, in response to determining that the client is authorized to access the file, a re-encrypted file encryption key to the client to enable the client to access the file. Various other methods, systems, and computer-readable media are also disclosed.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A computer-implemented method for encrypting files, at least a portion of the method being performed by a computing device comprising at least one processor, the method comprising: detecting an event within a network that triggers an encryption of a file on the network, performing, in response to detecting the event: encrypting the file to a file encryption key, and encrypting the file encryption key to a public key of a source of the file; receiving, from a client, a file access request that includes the encrypted file encryption key; and performing, in response to determining that the client is authorized to access the file: initiating a decryption of the encrypted file encryption key using a private key of the source of the file; re-encrypting the file encryption key using a public key of the client; and transmitting the re-encrypted file encryption key to the client to enable the client to access the file, 2. The computer -implemented method of claim 1, wherein the event within the network is detected by at least one of: a data loss prevention system; and a cloud access security broker. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein the file encryption key comprises a symmetrical file encryption key. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein the file enciyption key is randomly generated. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, wherein initiating the decryption of the encrypted file enciyption key using the private key of the source of the file comprises requesting that the source of the file perform the decryption. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 5, wherein requesting that the source of the file perform the decryption is performed without exposing the private key of the source of the file to a cloud computing network that is external to the source. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim I, wherein re-encrypting the file encryption key using the public key of the client is performed in response to receiving the public key from the client as part of the file access request. 8. The computer-implemented method of claim 1, further comprising transmitting to the client, in response to determining that the client is authorized to access the file, a cloud security policy that governs access to the file. 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, further comprising dynamically updating the cloud security policy based on at least one of: changing contents of the file; an identity of the client; a time zone in which the client is located; and a geolocation of the client. 10. The computer-implemented method of claim 8, wherein the cloud security policy specifies a limit on at least one of: printing the file; copy and paste access to contents of the file; editing of the file; and offline viewing of the file. 11. A system for detecting security incidents, the system comprising: a detection module, stored in memory, that detects an event within a network that triggers an encryption of a file on the network; a performance module, stored in memory, that performs, in response to detecting the event: encrypting the file to a file encryption key, and encrypting the file encryption key to a public key of a source of the file; 

 a reception module, stored in memory, that receives, from a client, a file access request that includes the encrypted file encryption key; wherein the performance module performs, in response to determining that the client is authorized to access the file: initiating a decryption of the encrypted file encryption key using a private key of the source of the file; re-encrypting the file enciyption key using a public key of the client; and transmitting the re-encrypted file encryption key to the client to enable the client to access the file; and at least one physical processor configured to execute the detection module, the performance module, and the reception module. 12. The system of claim 1 1 , wherein the event within the network is detected by at least one of: a data loss prevention system; and a cloud access security broker, 13. The system of claim 1 1, wherein the file enciyption key comprises a symmetrical file encryption key. 14. The system of claim 11, wherein the file encryption key is randomly generated. 15. The system of claim 11, wherein the performance module initiates the decryption of the encrypted file enciyption key using the private key of the source of the file by requesting that the source of the file perform the decryption. 16. The system of claim 15, wherein the performance module requests that the source of the file perform the decryption without exposing the private key of the source of the file to a cloud computing network that is external to the source. 17. The system of claim 1 1, wherein the performance module re-encrypts the file encryption key using the public key of the client in response to receiving the public key from the client as part of the file access request. 18. The system of claim 1 1 , wherein the performance module transmits to the client, in response to determining that the client is authorized to access the file, a cloud security policy that governs access to the file, 19. The system of claim 18, wherein the performance module dynamically updates the cloud security policy based on at least one of: changing contents of the fil e; an identity of the client; a time zone in which the client is located; and a seolocation of the client. 20. A non-transitory computer-readable medium comprising one or more computer- readable instructions that, when executed by at least one processor of a computing device, cause the computing device to: detect an event within a network that triggers an encryption of a file on the network; perform, in response to detecting the event: encrypting the file to a file encryption key; and encrypting the file encryption key to a public key of a source of the file; receive, from a client, a file access request that includes the encrypted file encryption key; and perform, in response to determining that the client is authorized to access the file: initiating a decryption of the encrypted file encryption key using a private key of the source of the file; re-encrypting the file encryption key using a public key of the client; and transmitting the re-encrypted file encryption key to the client to enable the client to access the file.
